title: 10 best herbal tinctures for upset stomach
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for upset stomach are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs, which are used to treat digestive issues such as nausea, bloating, and indigestion. These tinctures work by stimulating the production of digestive enzymes, reducing inflammation, and soothing the digestive tract. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with upset stomach include ginger, chamomile, and peppermint. Ginger, for instance, is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nausea and vomiting. Chamomile, on the other hand, has calming effects that can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which often exacerbate digestive issues. Peppermint, meanwhile, helps to relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing spasms and pain. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, improving their overall quality of life and ability to engage in daily activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tincture helps with upset stomach because it contains active compounds, such as absinthin and anthemic, that have been shown to stimulate digestive function and reduce inflammation in the gut. These properties can help alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, including nausea, bloating, and cramping. Additionally, wormwood has antimicrobial and antiparasitic properties, which may help eliminate harmful bacteria or parasites that can contribute to digestive issues. By promoting healthy digestion and eliminating harmful pathogens, wormwood tincture can provide relief for individuals experiencing upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Wormwood tincture can help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that often accompany an upset stomach. This is due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Carminative: Wormwood tincture promotes the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, alleviating bloating and discomfort associated with upset stomach. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as camphor, limonene, and pinene.

Choleretic: Wormwood tincture stimulates the production and release of bile from the gallbladder, which aids in digestion and can help alleviate symptoms of upset stomach. The main active constituent responsible for this property is absinthin.
Antimicrobial: Wormwood tincture can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the digestive tract that may contribute to upset stomach. This is due to the presence of compounds like thujone and isothujone.

Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tincture can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of upset stomach. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Wormwood leaves are commonly used in medicinal tea for upset stomach due to their bitter properties that can help stimulate digestion.
Flowers: The flowers of wormwood plant are used in tea to soothe digestive issues, they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stomach inflammation.
Seeds: Wormwood seeds are used in tea to alleviate stomach cramps and spasms, they contain compounds that can help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract.
Buds: The buds of wormwood plant are used in tea for their calming effects on the stomach, they contain volatile oils that can help reduce bloating and gas.
Bark: Wormwood bark is used in tea for its ability to soothe stomach lining, it contains tannins that can help protect the stomach from acid and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried wormwood herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the herbs, ensuring they are completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid herbs.
Store the finished tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use 1-2 mL as needed for upset stomach.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry tincture helps with upset stomach because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate cramping and pain in the stomach. Additionally, barberry has been used historically as a bitter digestive tonic, which can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, improving overall digestion and absorption of nutrients. This can help prevent upset stomach and promote a healthy gut. Overall, barberry tincture can provide natural relief for various digestive issues, including upset stomach, bloating, and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Barberry tincture can help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with upset stomach, due to the presence of alkaloids such as berberine.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help to tighten and tone the tissues of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and irritation, thanks to the tannins present in barberry.

Cholagogue: Barberry tincture stimulates the production and release of bile from the liver and gallbladder, aiding in digestion and the breakdown of fats, which can help alleviate upset stomach caused by poor digestion or fatty foods, due to the presence of alkaloids such as berberine.

Antiemetic: Barberry tincture can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of upset stomach, by reducing gastric motility and inhibiting the action of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that plays a role in nausea and vomiting, due to the presence of isoquinoline alkaloids such as berberine.

Anti-inflammatory: Barberry tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from upset stomach caused by inflammatory conditions such as gastritis or enteritis, due to the presence of alkaloids such as berberine and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Barberry roots are rich in berberine, a compound that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and relieve upset stomach.
Bark: The bark of the barberry plant contains berberine and other alkaloids, which can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce symptoms of digestive discomfort.
Berries: Barberry berries are a good source of antioxidants and can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief for an upset stomach.
Leaves: Barberry leaves contain tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe an upset stomach.
Stems: The stems of the barberry plant contain berberine, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and provide relief for an upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried barberry bark and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the bark, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the specified time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or muslin, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from direct sunlight. Recommended dosage for upset stomach is 1-2 teaspoons, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tincture helps with upset stomach because it contains anti-spasmodic and carminative properties that help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, gas, and cramps. The active compounds in caraway, including carvol and carvenone, work to relax the smooth muscle lining of the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and promoting the expulsion of gas. This herbal remedy has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various gastrointestinal disturbances, providing natural relief for an upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Caraway tincture helps relax gastrointestinal muscles, alleviating stomach cramps and spasms. This property is important for upset stomachs because it can help reduce discomfort and pain caused by muscle contractions. Active constituents responsible for this effect include carvol and limonene.

Carminative: Caraway tincture promotes the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and flatulence. This property is crucial for upset stomachs as it helps alleviate symptoms of bloating, discomfort, and pain caused by gas buildup. The active constituents responsible for this effect are carvol and flavonoids.

Digestive stimulant: Caraway tincture enhances digestive secretions, promoting better nutrient absorption and reducing symptoms of indigestion. This property is important for upset stomachs because it can help improve overall digestion and reduce symptoms like heartburn, nausea, and fullness. Active constituents contributing to this effect include carvol, limonene, and flavonoids.

Anxiolytic: Caraway tincture has mild sedative and calming effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may exacerbate upset stomachs. This property is essential for upset stomachs because stress and anxiety can worsen digestive symptoms and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this effect include carvol and limonene.

Antimicrobial: Caraway tincture inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause gastrointestinal infections and upset stomachs. This property is important for upset stomachs because it can help prevent and treat bacterial infections that may contribute to digestive issues. Active constituents responsible for this effect include carvone and limonene.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for upset stomach due to their carminative properties, which help to relieve bloating and gas.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the caraway tincture plant contain volatile oils and have been used in teas to soothe digestive discomfort and promote healthy digestion.
Leaves: The leaves of the caraway tincture plant have been used in tea to provide relief from stomach aches, as they contain anti-spasmodic compounds that help to relax the stomach muscles.
Flowers: Caraway flowers are rich in antioxidants and have been used in tea to support overall digestive health, providing relief from symptoms of upset stomach.
Roots: Caraway roots have been used in tea for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried caraway seeds and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100-proof vodka or rum over the caraway seeds, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle or jar.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and dosage instructions (typically 1-2 mL up to three times a day).

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peruvian bark tincture helps with upset stomach because it contains powerful alkaloids, such as quinine and cinchonine, which have anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing properties. These alkaloids can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate cramps and spasms in the stomach and intestines. Additionally, Peruvian bark has been shown to have antimicrobial and antiprotozoal effects, which can help eliminate the bacteria or parasites that may be causing the upset stomach. By addressing these underlying causes, Peruvian bark tincture can provide effective relief from upset stomach and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peruvian bark tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as tannins and alkaloids, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, providing relief from upset stomach and associated symptoms like cramps and pain.

Astringent: The astringent properties of Peruvian bark, due to its tannin content, help to constrict and tighten the tissues of the stomach lining, reducing the secretion of digestive juices and providing relief from acidity and irritation.

Antispasmodic: Peruvian bark tincture has antispasmodic properties, thanks to its alkaloids like catechin, that help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, thereby alleviating stomach cramps and spasms.

Amoebicidal: The presence of alkaloids like berberine in Peruvian bark tincture exhibits amoebicidal properties, which can help eliminate harmful microorganisms causing gastrointestinal infections, further aiding in the treatment of upset stomach and associated diarrhea.

Prokinetic: Peruvian bark tincture can stimulate gastric motility, thanks to its alkaloids, acting as a prokinetic agent. This property helps move food and fluids through the digestive system more efficiently, reducing the likelihood of stomach discomfort and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the Peruvian bark tincture plant is used to make medicinal tea for upset stomach due to its anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the Peruvian bark tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for upset stomach as they contain alkaloids that help soothe the digestive system.

Roots: The roots of the Peruvian bark tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for upset stomach as they contain quinine, which has been shown to have antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the Peruvian bark tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for upset stomach as they contain tannins that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Peruvian bark into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the bark, ensuring it is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean jar, discarding the solid pieces.
Label the jar with the contents and date, and take 1/4 teaspoon up to 3 times a day as needed for upset stomach.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coptis chinensis tincture helps with upset stomach because it contains high levels of berberine, a powerful alkaloid that has been shown to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and anti-diarrheal properties. This makes it effective in treating stomach upset caused by bacterial infections, inflammation, or digestive issues. Additionally, Coptis chinensis has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various gastrointestinal disorders, including nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Its astringent properties can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms of an upset stomach. Overall, Coptis chinensis tincture is a natural and effective remedy for treating stomach upset and promoting digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Coptis chinensis tincture helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate discomfort and irritation in the stomach. This property is important for upset stomach because inflammation can exacerbate symptoms and prolong recovery. The active constituents responsible for this property include berberine and palmatine.

Antiemetic: Coptis chinensis tincture helps alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of an upset stomach. This property is important for upset stomach because nausea and vomiting can lead to dehydration and further discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include berberine and coptisine.

Antibacterial: Coptis chinensis tincture has been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, such as H. pylori, which can contribute to stomach ulcers and other digestive issues. This property is important for upset stomach because bacterial infections can cause or worsen stomach symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include berberine and jatrorrhizine.

Antispasmodic: Coptis chinensis tincture helps relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping. This property is important for upset stomach because spasms and cramping can cause pain and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include palmatine and epiberberine.

Astringent: Coptis chinensis tincture helps tighten and tone the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing secretions and inflammation. This property is important for upset stomach because excessive secretions and inflammation can exacerbate symptoms and prolong recovery. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and berberine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of berberine, which can help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria, making it effective in treating upset stomach.
Rhizomes: Rich in alkaloids, they can help stimulate digestion and soothe gastrointestinal discomfort.
Leaves: Contain various flavonoids and essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, they can help soothe digestive discomfort and promote healthy digestion.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Coptis chinensis root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100 proof vodka or grain alcohol over the chopped root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the finished tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years. Take 1-2 mL up to three times daily for upset stomach.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cilantro tincture helps with upset stomach because it contains natural oils and compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and carminative properties. These properties can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, alleviate cramps and spasms, and expel gas from the stomach and intestines. As a result, cilantro tincture can provide quick and effective relief from symptoms of upset stomach, such as nausea, bloating, and abdominal pain. Moreover, cilantro has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to promote digestion and treat gastrointestinal disorders, making it a safe and time-tested remedy for upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Cilantro tincture can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for upset stomach as inflammation can exacerbate discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Carminative property: Cilantro tincture aids in expelling gas from the stomach and intestines, which is crucial for upset stomach as it helps alleviate bloating, cramps, and other symptoms caused by gas buildup. The active constituents responsible for this property include essential oils such as borneol, linalool, and geraniol.

Anti-emetic property: Cilantro tincture can help reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of upset stomach. The active constituents responsible for this property include cilantro's alkaloids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Antibacterial property: Cilantro tincture can help inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, which is important for upset stomach as it can help prevent or treat bacterial infections that can cause digestive issues. The active constituents responsible for this property include cilantro's essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant property: Cilantro tincture can help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to upset stomach. The active constituents responsible for this property include cilantro's flavonoids, phenolic acids, and vitamins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the cilantro plant are used to make medicinal tea for upset stomach due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and bloating.

Seeds: The seeds of the cilantro plant, also known as coriander seeds, are used in medicinal tea for upset stomach as they contain antispasmodic compounds that help to relax the stomach muscles and alleviate cramps.

Stems: The stems of the cilantro plant are used in medicinal tea for upset stomach as they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe an upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh cilantro leaves and stems, place them in a clean jar, then add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof).
Tightly seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the steepening period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, pressing the cilantro solids to extract all the liquid.
Transfer the resulting greenish-brown liquid (cilantro tincture) into a dark glass dropper bottle.
For upset stomach, take 20-30 drops of cilantro tincture in a small amount of water or directly under the tongue, 3 times a day or as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with upset stomach because it contains curcumin, a compound with well-known anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. This active ingredient aids in reducing inflammation in the gut lining, alleviating irritation, and improving overall digestion. Furthermore, turmeric tincture stimulates the production of bile in the liver, which is essential for breaking down fats and preventing feelings of bloating and discomfort. As a result, incorporating turmeric tincture into your routine can provide soothing relief for an upset stomach and promote a healthy gastrointestinal system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituent curcumin, helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating upset stomach.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic property of turmeric tincture, also attributed to curcumin, aids in relieving pain and discomfort caused by upset stomach.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in turmeric tincture, primarily from curcumin, helps protect the gastrointestinal system from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall digestive health.

Antispasmodic: Turmeric tincture's antispasmodic property, derived from curcumin and other compounds, helps relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with upset stomach.

Digestive aid: Turmeric tincture stimulates the production of bile in the liver, which is essential for fat digestion and absorption, promoting a healthy digestive process and alleviating upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's underground stem or rhizome is used to make medicinal tea for upset stomach due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves are used in making medicinal tea for upset stomach because they contain curcumin, which helps in soothing the stomach lining and reducing inflammation.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers are used in making medicinal tea as they contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help in reducing stomach acid and providing relief from acid reflux.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds are used in making medicinal tea for upset stomach due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help in reducing bloating and gas.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh turmeric root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring the turmeric is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the solid turmeric pieces.
Store the resulting tincture in a glass dropper bottle and take 1-2 dropperfuls as needed for upset stomach.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild yam tincture helps with upset stomach because it contains compounds called diosgenin and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate muscle spasms in the intestines, thereby providing relief from symptoms such as cramping, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, wild yam tincture can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can improve nutrient absorption and overall digestive function. By promoting a healthy digestive system, wild yam tincture can help prevent and manage upset stomach and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wild yam tincture can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from upset stomach and associated symptoms like cramps and bloating. This property is due to the presence of steroidal saponins, such as diosgenin.

Antispasmodic: Wild yam tincture helps alleviate muscle spasms, including those in the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to an upset stomach. The compound diosgenin is responsible for this antispasmodic effect.

Carminative: Wild yam tincture can help expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with an upset stomach. The essential oils and steroidal saponins present in wild yam contribute to this carminative property.

Demulcent: Wild yam tincture forms a soothing film over mucous membranes, protecting them from irritation and inflammation that can cause an upset stomach. The mucilage content in wild yam provides this demulcent effect.

Cholagogue: Wild yam tincture promotes the flow of bile from the liver, aiding in digestion and reducing the likelihood of an upset stomach. The steroidal saponins, such as diosgenin, are responsible for this cholagogue effect.
remedy_parts: Roots: The wild yam roots are commonly used in medicinal teas for upset stomach due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of wild yam are rich in steroidal saponins, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and reducing stomach discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of wild yam contain various alkaloids and flavonoids, which can help alleviate stomach aches and promote healthy digestion.
Flowers: Infusing wild yam flowers in tea can help relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing symptoms of upset stomach and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of wild yam root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Add 1 pint of 100 proof vodka or grain alcohol to the jar, ensuring the root is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid from the root.
Store the final tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and take 1-2 mL up to three times daily for upset stomach.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cardamom tincture helps with upset stomach because it contains volatile oils and compounds that have been shown to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. The active ingredients in cardamom, such as cineole and limonene, work together to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, relax gastrointestinal muscles, and soothe irritation in the stomach lining. This herbal remedy has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, making it a natural and effective option for those seeking relief from an upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Cardamom tincture helps to relieve stomach discomfort by reducing gas and bloating, thanks to the active constituents such as volatile oils like cineole and alpha-terpinene.
Spasmolytic: It can help relax gastrointestinal spasms, providing relief from cramps and pain, due to the presence of volatile oils and various flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Cardamom tecture can decrease inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating irritation and promoting healing, by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals, partly due to compounds like limonene and cineole.
Antacid: Cardamom tincture can neutralize stomach acid, preventing acid reflux and heartburn, by containing compounds like manganese and calcium that help balance stomach pH.
Digestive stimulant: It promotes the production of digestive enzymes, enhancing nutrient absorption and overall digestion, due to the presence of essential oils, vitamins, and minerals.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Cardamom seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for upset stomach due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and bloating.
Flowers: The flowers of the cardamom tincture plant are used in tea to soothe digestive issues, thanks to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.
Leaves: Cardamom leaves have been known to aid in digestion and alleviate stomach discomfort, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the cardamom tincture plant are rich in volatile oils, which can help settle an upset stomach and provide relief from nausea.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh cardamom seeds or 1/2 cup of dried seeds and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum with at least 40% alcohol content over the cardamom seeds, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the recommended time, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle, seal it, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for upset stomach.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Boneset tincture helps with upset stomach because it contains anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties that can soothe an irritated digestive tract and reduce muscle spasms in the intestines, thereby alleviating symptoms such as cramping, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, boneset tincture has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including indigestion, due to its ability to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which can improve nutrient absorption and overall digestive function. The herbal constituents in boneset tincture, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, also have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress-related digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Boneset tincture can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from upset stomach symptoms, due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antispasmodic: Boneset tincture can help alleviate muscle spasms and cramping in the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to an upset stomach, thanks to the relaxing effects of its antispasmodic constituents, such as polyphenols and alkaloids.

Diaphoretic: Boneset tincture promotes sweating, which can help the body eliminate toxins and reduce fever, and this property can support overall digestive health and alleviate upset stomach symptoms, as diaphoretic compounds like polyphenols and sesquiterpene lactones stimulate sweat glands.

Carminative: Boneset tincture can help expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort, by stimulating the production of digestive enzymes, thanks to its carminative constituents, including volatile oils and flavonoids.

Bitter Stimulant: Boneset tincture can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, improving overall digestion and absorption of nutrients, and this can help prevent and alleviate upset stomach symptoms, due to the presence of bitter compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of boneset tincture plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for upset stomach due to their anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the boneset tincture plant are rich in essential oils and have been traditionally used to soothe digestive discomforts and promote healthy digestion.
Flowers: The flowers of the boneset tincture plant contain flavonoids that have anti-spasmodic properties, making them useful for reducing muscle spasms in the digestive tract.
Seeds: The seeds of the boneset tincture plant are a good source of antioxidants and have been used to treat gastrointestinal issues such as bloating and cramping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest boneset herb (Eupatorium perfoliatum) leaves and flowers, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides, then rinse and dry.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried boneset herb to increase surface area for extraction, then place in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 1 pint (16 ounces) of 80-100 proof vodka or grain alcohol over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged, then secure the lid and shake gently.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily, then strain using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer into a clean, dry glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and recommended dosage (typically 1-2 mL up to three times a day), then store in a cool, dark place or refrigerate for up to a year.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat upset stomach with herbal tinctures is by using ginger or chamomile. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stomach irritation and nausea. Chamomile, on the other hand, contains calming compounds that relax the stomach muscles, alleviating discomfort and cramps. To use, mix 10-20 drops of the tincture in a glass of water and drink it slowly. Remember to always follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upset stomach that are treated with herbal tinctures include indigestion, acid reflux, and irritable bowel syndrome. Tinctures made from herbs such as ginger, chamomile, and licorice have been used to alleviate symptoms like nausea, bloating, and abdominal pain. Peppermint and caraway oils are also used in tincture form to soothe digestive issues. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment, especially if you have a medical condition or are taking other medications.
